GIN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO BOTH THE FIRST AND SIXTH CAUSES OF ACTION. TO THE EXTENT THAT THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED ON AN ALLEGED ILLEGAL DETENTION OR EXCESSIVE FORCE, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED, NOR DOES IT APPEAR HE IS ABLE TO ALLEGE, ANY VIOLENCE OR COERCION INDEPENDENT FROM WHAT IS INHERENT IN THE DETENTION AND FORCE ITSELF. TO THE EXTENT THAT THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED ON AN ALLEGED VIOLATION OF DUE PROCESS, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED, NOT DOES IT APPEAR THAT HE IS ABLE TO ALLEGE, THAT HIS DUE PROCESS RIGHTS WERE INTERFERED WITH BY ANY THREAT, INTIMIDATION OR COERCION. PLAINTIFF'S SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS BECAUSE HE HAS NOT ALLEGED, NOR DOES IT APPEAR THAT HE IS ABLE TO ALLEGE, THE EXISTENCE OF ANY CONTROVERSY FOR WHICH DECLARATORY RELIEF IS EITHER NECESSARY OR APPROPRIATE. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF BRET CORNELL'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUGGESTION FOR SUA SPONTE RECONSIDERATION IS GRANTED. THE COURT EXERCISES ITS DISCRETION TO RECONSIDER THE PORTION OF THE ORDER FILED JULY 20, 2012 GRANTING SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANTS ON THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND, UPON RECONSIDERATION, VACATES THAT PORTION OF THE ORDER AND ENTERS A NEW ORDER DENYING SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION. BENDER EXPLICITLY HOLDS THAT "WHERE AN ARREST IS UNLAWFUL AND EXCESSIVE FORCE IS APPLIED IN MAKING THE ARREST, THERE HAS BEEN COERCION 'INDEPENDENT FROM THE COERCION INHERENT IN THE WRONGFUL DETENTION ITSELF.'" (EMPHASIS IN ORIGINAL). THIS HOLDING, WHETHER SEEN AS A CLARIFICATION OF SHOYOYE OR NEW LAW, DEMONSTRATES THAT THE PRIOR RULING IS IN ERROR AND THUS THIS COURT EXERCISE ITS DISCRETION TO CORRECT ERRORS OF LAW PRIOR TO ENTRY OF THE FINAL JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT IS GIVEN PERMISSION TO FILE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON SHORTENED TIME. HEARING IS SET FOR OCTOBER 8, 2013 AT 9:00 A.M. IN DEPT. 302. MOTION MUST BE FILED BY SEPTEMBER 13, 2013; OPPOSITION TO BE FILED BY SEPT. 27, 2013; REPLY TO BE FILED BY OCTOBER 4, 2013. SERVICE TO BE DONE THRU E-MAIL. THE SCOPE OF THE DEPOSITION IS LIMITED TO: (A) THE WITNESS' PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE CONCERNING THE FACTS AND CIRCUMSTANCES OF THE PLAINTIFF'S CASE AND HIS INPUT OR ACTIONS; (B) THE ISSUANCE OF THE TERMINATION MEMORANDUM AND TERMINATION LETTER, AND ANY CONVERSATIONS OR COMMUNICATIONS IN CONNECTION WITH THE TERMINATION; (C) POLICIES AND PROCEDURES ONLY WITH RESPECT TO THE FACTS OF THE PLAINTIFF'S CASE AND WITH RESPECT TO THE PLAINTIFF; PLAINTIFF MAY NOT CONDUCT A GENERAL INQUIRY INTO ADMINISTRATIVE OR HR POLICIES WITH RESPECT TO THIRD PERSONS OR HYPOTHETICAL SITUATIONS; (D) PLAINTIFF MAY NOT DEPOSE THE WITNESS CONCERNING THE RECORDS OR HISTORY OF THE OTHER OFFICERS INVOLVED IN THE CASE WITHIN THE SCOPE OF A PITCHESS INQUIRY. THIS IS NOT A RULING ON A PITCHESS REQUEST BUT A SUBJECT FOR A LATER MOTION OR DECISION BY THE COURT. HOWEVER, IF THERE WERE CONVERSATIONS BETWEEN CHIEF GASCON AND ANY OTHER OFFICERS CONCERNING THE PLAINTIFF OR THE FACTS OF THE CASE, THAT INQUIRY MAY BE PURSUED; AND (E) THE DEPOSITION SHALL NOT EXCEED 2 HOURS, UNLESS PLAINTIFF SHOWS GOOD CAUSE. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-08-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MICHAEL HADDAD, ESQ., 510.452.5500, FOR PLAINTIFF BRET CORNELL.
MARGARET BAUMGARTNER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, 510.554.3800, FOR THE DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, et al.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs POLICE OFFICER RICHARD BODISCO, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE OFFICER DAVID BRANDT, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE OFFICER JESSE FARRELL, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE SERGEANT WALLACE GIN, INDIVIDUALLY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS Granted as to defendants Richard Bodisco and Jesse Farrell and denied as to defendants David Brandt, Wallace Gin and the City and County of San Francisco. As to Bodisco and Farrell, even assuming that they wrongfully detained and/or wrongfully arrested plaintiff Bret Cornell, the undisputed material facts establish that they did not personally use excessive force nor were they in a position, by their intercession or other participation, to have prevented or mitigated the use of excessive force by other officers. As to Brandt, Gin and the City, there are material issues of disputed fact whether they detained Cornell without reasonable suspicion, arrested him without probable cause and, in the course of detaining and/or arresting him, used excessive force. Order signed in open court. Judge: HAROLD KAHN; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Plaintiff Bret Cornell'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint Or In The Alternative Suggestion For Sua Sponte Reconsideration
Off Calendar Sep-04-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL J. HADDAD & ATTY. GENEVIEVE K. GUERTIN OF HADDAD & SHERWIN, TEL. NO. (510) 452-5500 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARGARET W. BAUMGARTNER, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-3858 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiff Bret Cornell'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint Or In The Alternative Suggestion For Sua Sponte Reconsideration is Granted. The court exercises its discretion to reconsider the portion of the order filed July 20, 2012 granting summary adjudication in favor of defendants on the first cause of action and, upon reconsideration, vacates that portion of the order and enters a new order denying summary adjudication on the first cause of action. Bender explicitly holds that "where an arrest is unlawful and excessive force is applied in making the arrest, there has been coercion 'independent from the coercion inherent in the wrongful detention itself.'" (Emphasis in original). This holding, whether seen as a clarification of Shoyoye or new law, demonstrates that the prior ruling is in error and thus this court exercise its discretion to correct errors of law prior to entry of the final judgment. Defendant is given permission to file Summary Adjudication on shortened time. Hearing is set for October 8, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 302. Motion must be filed by September 13, 2013; Opposition to be filed by Sept. 27, 2013; Reply to be filed by October 4, 2013. Service to be done thru e-mail. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, CHIEF OF POLICE GEORGE GASCON, POLICE OFFICER RICHARD BODISCO, POLICE OFFICER DAVID BRANDT, POLICE OFFICER JESSE FARRELL AND SGT. GIN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend as to both the first and sixth causes of action. To the extent that the first cause of action is based on an alleged illegal detention or excessive force, plaintiff has not alleged, nor does it appear he is able to allege, any violence or coercion independent from what is inherent in the detention and force itself. To the extent that the first cause of action is based on an alleged violation of due process, plaintiff has not alleged, not does it appear that he is able to allege, that his due process rights were interfered with by any threat, intimidation or coercion. Plaintiff's sixth cause of action fails because he has not alleged, nor does it appear that he is able to allege, the existence of any controversy for which declaratory relief is either necessary or appropriate. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-03-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, POLICE OFFICER RICHARD BODISCO, POLICE OFFICER DAVID BRANDT, POLICE OFFICER JESSE FARRELL, POLICE SERGEANT WALLACE GIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES.
Appearances: Michael J. Haddad of Haddad & Sherwin (510.452.5500) for plaintiff and responding party; Margaret W. Baumgartner of the Office of the City Attorney (554.3859) for defendants and moving parties.
Argued and the court ruled as follows: motion for summary judgment is denied and the motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 1 and 10, granted as to the City only as to issue 8, off calendar as moot as to issues 2, 3 and 4 and as to issue 9 as to the City only, denied as to issues 5, 6 and 7, and as to the issues 8 and 9 as to the City employees only. As to issue 1, the alleged excessive force used against Plaintiff was inherent in the detention itself. As to issues 5, 6 and 7, there are triable issues whether there was reasonable suspicion to detain, reasonable circumstances to draw a gun, and probable cause to arrest. As to issues 8 and 9, the City cannot interfere with its own economic relationship with Plaintiff, but there are triable issues whether Defendants Bodisco, Brandt, Farrell, and Gin so interfered and did so by engaging in wrongful conduct. As to issue 10, Mr. Gascon has satisfied his burden of production and plaintiff has not presented any evidence showing that Mr. Gascon had personal involvement in the incident. Further, as to issue 10, the court grants 10 days leave to amend to allege, if plaintiff can do so in good faith, ultimate facts supporting a procedural due process claim against Mr. Gascon and/or a negligent hiring, training and supervision claim against Mr. Gascon. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR # 5976

Notice Of Motion And Otion To Compel The Deposition Of Deft George Gascon; Memo Of P&A
Off Calendar Jun-12-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gina Altomare, Esq., from Haddad & Sherwin, 510-452-5500, for plaintiff and moving party
Margaret W. Baumgartner, DCA, from The Office of the City Attorney, for defendant and opposing party CCSF

Argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as modified.
PLAINTIFF BRET CORNELL Notice Of Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Deft George Gascon. The court adopts the recommendation of Pro Tem Rosenquist as follows: Granted. The scope of the deposition is limited to: (a) the witness' personal knowledge concerning the facts and circumstances of the plaintiff's case and his input or actions; (b) the issuance of the termination memorandum and termination letter, and any conversations or communications in connection with the termination; (c) policies and procedures only with respect to the facts of the plaintiff's case and with respect to the plaintiff; Plaintiff may not conduct a general inquiry into administrative or HR policies with respect to third persons or hypothetical situations; (d) Plaintiff may not depose the witness concerning the records or history of the other officers involved in the case within the scope of a Pitchess inquiry. This is not a ruling on a Pitchess request but a subject for a later motion or decision by the court. However, if there were conversations between Chief Gascon and any other officers concerning the plaintiff or the facts of the case, that inquiry may be pursued; and (e) The deposition shall not exceed 2 hours, unless plaintiff shows good cause. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
